decisions_or_constraints: Task generation must remain artifact-only; no source code, tests, package scripts, implementation files, secrets, or task-generation files outside this handoff path were modified.

Feature decomposition should preserve the user-facing priority order from spec.md: Primary Scenario - Fast Local Verification first, Secondary Scenario - Layered PR Verification second, then edge-case/high-risk verification and cross-cutting output semantics.

Capability phases in tasks.md are technically coherent and mostly match plan.md Phase 1 through Phase 8, but user-story labels required by the task-generation checklist are absent from user-story/capability implementation tasks.

The feature is capability-heavy rather than UI-story-heavy; downstream packets should keep capability names as phase anchors while adding story/capability trace labels so implementers can prove independent delivery.
MP-011 remains deferred by design; do not add an expansion-ready test impact graph in this task package.

SC-001: Covered by T005 quality:fast script entry, T009 core-smoke runtime measurement, T027 final fast-lane validation, and Phase 1/3 join points.
SC-002: Covered by T010 PR lane ordering, T011 layered execution filtering, T020-T021 changed-line coverage handling, T027 final verify validation, and Phase 4/7 join points.
SC-003: Covered by T006 fastLaneTests impact-report section, T014 escalatedChecks/risk note output, and Phase 2/5 join points.
SC-004: Covered by T013 risk escalation rules, T014 escalatedChecks output, T015 impactRequiredCheck wiring, T024 risk escalation tests, and Phase 5 join point.
SC-005: Covered by T016 report type fields, T017 report value assignment, T018 output banners, T025 output semantics tests, and Phase 6 join point.
SC-006: Covered by T022-T026 routing regression tests across fast mode, changed-area selection, risk escalation, output semantics, and coverage strategy.
MP-001: Covered by layered task package plus T027 final validation; speed improvements are paired with regression/routing tests and guardrails.
MP-002: Covered by T009, T027, and explicit runtime acceptance targets for under-1-minute fast lane and under-5-minute routine PR/verify.
MP-003: Covered by T001-T002 guardrail reads and all implementation tasks extending scripts/quality-gate and scripts/pr instead of a parallel system.
MP-004: Covered by T006-T008 and T022-T023 for changed-area tests plus core smoke.
MP-005: Covered by T005 anti-goal, T010 acceptance criteria, and package/script constraints preserving bun run verify as PR readiness entrypoint.
MP-006: Covered by T013-T015 and T024 for conditional E2E/live-provider/release escalation.
MP-007: Covered by T021 coverage ratchet acceptance criteria, T022-T026 regression tests, and T027 quality validation.
MP-008: Covered by anti-goals across implementation tasks, T019-T021 coverage preservation, and Task Guardrail Index entries.
MP-009: Covered by T001-T002 read scopes and repeated required references to existing quality-gate and PR surfaces.
MP-010: Covered by T016-T018 and T025; fast reports must set readyForMerge false and display NOT PR READY.
MP-011: Explicitly deferred; T006 anti-goal forbids creating a new test impact graph and requires using existing area classification.
MP-012: Covered by T019-T021; plan resolved strategy as changed-line coverage for PR and full coverage for baseline/release, with runtime validation still required.

phase_recommendations.recommendation: Keep as a required setup/guardrail phase, but do not treat read-only confirmation tasks as user-story work.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: US1 / Capability 1: Fast Local Verification - Fast Mode Shell
phase_recommendations.rationale: This is the first independently valuable user-facing slice: contributors can invoke a fast lane.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: US1 / Capability 2: Fast Local Verification - Changed-Area Tests
phase_recommendations.rationale: Fast lane must run meaningful changed-area tests, not only policy/lint checks.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: US1 / Capability 3: Fast Local Verification - Core Smoke
phase_recommendations.rationale: Core smoke is part of the fast-lane acceptance signal and should remain before PR layering.
PR/verify layering depends on the fast-lane building blocks and preserves bun run verify as PR readiness.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: US2 + Edge Cases / Capability 5: Risk-Based Escalation
phase_recommendations.rationale: High-risk path escalation is part of PR readiness and edge-case coverage, not a standalone release slice.
Output labels protect both fast lane and PR verification from false readiness claims; add task labels tied to both US1 and US2 where practical.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: US2 / Layered PR Verification - Coverage Strategy
phase_recommendations.rationale: Changed-line coverage is a PR/verify concern and should remain after PR layering and before final regression validation.

phase_recommendations.recommended_phase: Validation Phase: Regression Coverage For All Stories
phase_recommendations.rationale: Tests cover all capabilities; keep after implementation dependencies but ensure each story has a before-or-with implementation test task where downstream execution requires TDD.

phase_recommendations.recommendation: Keep final validation, but split docs/quickstart update into an allowed artifact lane only if implementation execution explicitly permits spec artifact updates.

task_adjustments.issue: Missing checklist story labels for user-story/capability phase tasks.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Add labels such as [US1] for fast local verification tasks, [US2] for PR verification tasks, and use a clear cross-cutting marker in the task body for output semantics if the strict checklist format cannot represent multiple stories.

task_adjustments.issue: Read-only guardrail tasks have subjective acceptance criteria such as 'understood' and no concrete evidence output.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Keep the guardrail phase, but make completion executable by requiring a short notes artifact in the worker handoff or a named checkpoint field summarizing confirmed patterns.

task_adjustments.issue: Some verification commands rely on --dry-run or shell-specific time behavior that may not exist or may be platform-sensitive.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Verify exact command support before downstream execution; prefer repository-supported commands such as bun run quality:gate --mode fast when quality:fast is not yet available, and use PowerShell-compatible timing on Windows.

task_adjustments.issue: Task describes core-smoke lane kind as 'command' while plan.md says kind: 'sequential'.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Align wording with the actual existing LaneDefinition shape after inspecting modes.ts; avoid prescribing an invalid kind value.

task_adjustments.issue: RiskLevel type is placed in scripts/quality-gate/types.ts but risk classification lives in scripts/pr/change-policy.ts; downstream execution must confirm ownership before adding cross-module type coupling.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Keep these tasks but add context pointer to plan.md#boundary-ownership and require implementer to reuse existing type/export patterns rather than introducing circular imports.

task_adjustments.issue: All regression tests are placed after implementation, which may conflict with the project's TDD preference for behavior changes.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Either move narrow failing test tasks before their corresponding implementation phases or mark Phase 8 as consolidation tests while adding same-area test requirements inside Phases 1-7.

task_adjustments.issue: Agent value 'doc-updater' is not in the sp-tasks allowed agent roster.
task_adjustments.recommendation: Change agent to 'executor' or another allowed role, or remove the task if post-implementation quickstart updates are outside execution scope.

context_pointer_requirements: Every downstream packet should point to spec.md#scenarios-and-usage-paths for story priority and independent acceptance.
Every downstream packet should point to spec.md#success-criteria for SC-001 through SC-006 traceability.
Every downstream packet touching implementation should point to plan.md#implementation-constitution for architecture invariants, boundary ownership, and forbidden implementation drift.
Fast lane packets should point to plan.md#phase-1-fast-lane-mode-definition, plan.md#phase-2-changed-area-test-selection, and plan.md#phase-3-core-smoke-set.
PR layering packets should point to plan.md#phase-4-layered-pr-mode-refinement and plan.md#phase-7-coverage-strategy-implementation.
Risk escalation packets should point to plan.md#phase-5-risk-based-escalation and research.md#d-005-risk-based-escalation-for-heavy-checks.
Output semantics packets should point to plan.md#phase-6-output-semantics and research.md#d-006-output-semantics-implementation.
All packets should point to context.md#must-preserve-execution-constraints and context.md#affected-surfaces.
Coverage tasks should point to plan-contract.json must_preserve MP-012 and plan.md#locked-planning-decisions LD-004.
Regression test packets should point to tasks.md#success-criteria-coverage and tasks.md#must-preserve-invariants.
Packets must include forbidden paths: .env, credentials/secrets, unrelated source surfaces, and any task-generation artifact outside the packet's explicit write scope.
validation_evidence: spec.md defines two main scenarios in priority order: Primary Scenario - Fast Local Verification, then Secondary Scenario - Layered PR Verification, plus high-risk edge cases.
spec.md defines six capabilities: Fast Lane Definition, Changed-Area Test Selection, Core Smoke Set, Layered PR Mode, Risk-Based Escalation, and Output Semantics.
plan.md defines eight implementation phases that correspond to the six capabilities plus coverage strategy and regression testing.
tasks.md maps SC-001 through SC-006 in its Success Criteria Coverage table.
tasks.md maps MP-001 through MP-012 in its Must-Preserve Invariants table, with MP-011 explicitly deferred and MP-012 resolved to changed-line PR coverage plus full baseline/release coverage.
tasks.md currently has no [US1]/[US2] checklist labels on implementation tasks, despite the sp-tasks checklist requiring story labels for user story phase tasks.
tasks.md uses 'doc-updater' for T028, which is outside the allowed sp-tasks agent roster listed in the workflow skill.
concerns: Capability-based phases are workable for this feature but should be explicitly tied back to user stories so downstream implementation can prove independent delivery.

The current task graph is mostly serial despite claiming intra-phase parallel batches; several [P] candidates touch shared files such as scripts/quality-gate/types.ts or scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts across phases and should be scheduled with join points to avoid write conflicts.

Some commands in verify_commands may be aspirational until implementation creates quality:fast or supports --dry-run; downstream packets should validate command availability before treating them as acceptance commands.
The regression testing phase comes late; if strict TDD is required, task order should be adjusted so tests precede the behavior they verify.
recovery_hints: If analyze rejects missing story labels, relabel capability implementation tasks by story: [US1] for fast local verification, [US2] for layered PR verification, and keep cross-cutting output/coverage tasks with explicit body-level story traceability.
If analyze rejects non-executable guardrail tasks, convert T001-T002 into evidence-producing checkpoint tasks with a required handoff field rather than subjective 'understood' acceptance.
If analyze rejects command validity, run a quick script inventory against package.json and runner CLI options, then replace unsupported commands with existing narrow checks.
If analyze rejects agent roster drift, change T028 agent from doc-updater to executor or split documentation update into a separate allowed artifact task.
